meta.description: Use when constructing a prognosis nomogram from survival-related clinical predictors, exporting the nomogram bundle and C-index table, and optionally rendering the final nomogram PDF. NOT for: univariate/multivariable Cox feature screening, calibration curves, ROC analysis, decision-curve analysis, or non-survival outcomes.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated DOI, PMID, C-index values, or survival statistics; all values computed from provided clinical data via rms::cph.

practice_boundaries.detail: Explicitly not for clinical diagnosis; skill is scoped to nomogram construction from pre-selected predictors only.

methodological_ground.detail: rms::cph is the canonical Cox model implementation for nomogram construction in R; C-index computation is methodologically correct; minimum sample/event requirements prevent invalid model fitting.

code_usability.detail: All 6 R modules syntactically valid; withCallingHandlers/tryCatch pattern correct; qs serialization for portable bundle storage.

functional_suitability.note: Full coverage of nomogram construction, C-index computation, .qs bundle export, and PDF rendering. Two-mode design cleanly separates expensive fitting from plot iteration.

reliability.note: Validation for file existence, CSV format, min 20 complete samples, min 10 events; 9 distinct SKILL_* codes covering all failure modes including SKILL_ANALYSIS_ERROR for cph() failures.

performance_context.note: When-to-read table; references deferred; SKILL.md ~340 lines well within acceptable range; two-mode design enables lightweight plot rerenders.

agent_usability.note: Mode-based execution is clear; prerequisite section explicit; feedback design note: no append-only run_record/output_manifest like estimate and GSVA sibling skills — only session_info.txt.

human_usability.note: Specific trigger phrases (prognosis nomogram, C-index, pre-selected prognostic variables); strict CSV validation correct per Scene Override.

security.note: No hardcoded secrets; CSV and column validation present; missing explicit privacy note for clinical CSV input.

maintainability.note: 6-module R architecture; smoke test with included data and automated script; --overwrite flag enables clean test reruns.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision strong with pre-selected predictors qualifier; .qs bundle enables clean downstream composition with calibration-curve and plot-only skills; --overwrite guard for idempotency.

inputs.label: Build nomogram from age, stage, risk — 3 predictors, 1/2/3 year horizons
inputs.note: Full build: validate CSV -> complete-case filter -> cph() -> nomogram() -> C-index -> .qs + .rds + .xlsx. All 4 expected output files generated.

assertions.text: Nomogram bundle (data/Nomogram_list.qs) saved with all required objects
assertions.note: Bundle objects (nomogram, c_index, model, data, features, time_points) documented in Input Format section for plot mode.

assertions.text: C-index table (table/nomogram_c_index.xlsx) generated with metric and value columns
assertions.note: nomogram_c_index.xlsx schema documented with metric and value columns.

assertions.text: set.seed() applied before Cox model fitting
assertions.note: set.seed(options$seed) called in main.R before run_analysis().

assertions.text: session_info.txt written with build parameters for reproducibility
assertions.note: save_session_info called with full parameter list in run_analysis.R build branch.

assertions.text: No medical diagnosis or clinical recommendation made
assertions.note: When Not to Use explicitly excludes diagnosis models and clinical decision support.

inputs.label: Build nomogram with 4 predictors including categorical variables
inputs.note: 4 predictors including categorical (gender, treatment) converted to factors automatically. C-index computed for 4-predictor model.

assertions.text: Character predictors automatically converted to factors for Cox model fitting
assertions.note: Workflow Step 2 states: 'Convert character predictors to factors' in prepare_nomogram_data.

assertions.text: C-index computed correctly for 4-predictor model
assertions.note: C-index computation is model-agnostic; number of predictors does not affect the computation method.

assertions.text: At least 3 features requirement satisfied by 4 predictors
assertions.note: Input Format requirement: 'At least 3 prognostic features are required' — 4 features satisfies this.

assertions.text: SKILL_ANALYSIS_ERROR would surface if cph() fitting fails
assertions.note: SKILL_ANALYSIS_ERROR documented: 'cph() fitting, nomogram construction, or output writing failed'.

inputs.label: Clinical CSV with only 15 complete samples and 5 events — below minimum threshold
inputs.note: SKILL_INSUFFICIENT_DATA raised: requires >= 20 complete samples and >= 10 events. No partial model saved. Error message identifies specific requirements.

assertions.text: SKILL_INSUFFICIENT_DATA raised when fewer than 20 complete samples are present
assertions.note: Input Format states: 'At least 20 complete samples and at least 10 events are required after filtering incomplete rows.'

assertions.text: Error message identifies specific sample and event count requirements
assertions.note: SKILL_INSUFFICIENT_DATA error: 'Too few features, complete samples, or events' with solution 'Provide more valid predictors or samples'.

assertions.text: No partial nomogram model is saved when minimum requirements are not met
assertions.note: Validation fires before create_output_dirs and model fitting; no outputs written on validation failure.

inputs.label: Render nomogram PDF from existing .qs bundle with custom dimensions and font size
inputs.note: Plot mode: load .qs bundle -> validate required objects -> render PDF with custom width=12 and font_size=10.

assertions.text: Plot mode reads from .qs bundle without re-running Cox analysis
assertions.note: Mode branching in main.R: plot mode calls read_nomogram_bundle and save_nomogram_plot only.

assertions.text: Custom plot_width and font_size parameters applied to PDF rendering
assertions.note: --plot_width (-w) and --font_size (-F) documented CLI parameters; passed to save_nomogram_plot.

assertions.text: session_info.txt written in plot directory with plot parameters
assertions.note: save_session_info called with plot metadata in plot mode branch of run_analysis.R.

assertions.text: SKILL_FILE_NOT_FOUND raised if .qs bundle is missing
assertions.note: SKILL_FILE_NOT_FOUND documented: 'Input file does not exist'.

inputs.label: Build nomogram with 8 predictors, 1/3/5 year horizons, and 120-second timeout
inputs.note: 8 predictors accepted; custom years 1,3,5 parsed; timeout enforced at 120s via apply_time_limit.

assertions.text: 8 predictors accepted (minimum 3 required)
assertions.note: Feature count validation only enforces minimum of 3; no upper bound documented.

assertions.text: Custom years 1,3,5 correctly parsed and applied to nomogram survival functions
assertions.note: --years '1,3,5' documented; parse_year_arg function processes comma-separated string.

assertions.text: SKILL_TIMEOUT raised if 120-second limit is exceeded
assertions.note: apply_time_limit called with options$timeout_seconds; timeout error message hardcoded in main.R error handler.

assertions.text: No fabricated C-index values — all statistics computed from actual data
assertions.note: C-index computed from rms::cph fit on provided data; no hardcoded or interpolated values.

key_strengths: rms::cph is the methodologically correct foundation for Cox-based nomograms in R; C-index computation is standard and well-validated.
Two-mode separation (build vs plot) with .qs bundle enables efficient iteration on visualization without re-running expensive Cox fitting — excellent design for clinical researchers.
Comprehensive SKILL_* error table with 9 distinct codes including SKILL_ANALYSIS_ERROR covering cph() fitting failures that other skills may silently swallow.
--overwrite guard prevents accidental output directory corruption during repeated test runs.

recommendations.title: Missing append-only run_record and output_manifest
recommendations.problem: Unlike estimate-immune-score-analysis and gsva-analysis-and-visualization sibling skills, nomogram-construction does not maintain append-only run_record.txt or output_manifest.txt. Only session_info.txt is written, which is overwritten on each run.
recommendations.root_cause: recording.R module present in estimate and GSVA skills was not included in nomogram-construction. Only 6 modules present vs 7-9 in sibling skills.
recommendations.fix: Add recording.R module and call write_run_record and write_output_manifest at the end of both build and plot modes. Include mode, parameters, runtime, and output file list in each run record entry.

recommendations.title: Missing privacy advisory for clinical CSV input
recommendations.problem: Clinical CSV files for nomogram construction are likely to contain patient-level survival and clinical data. No privacy or anonymization advisory is present, unlike the GSVA sibling skill.
recommendations.root_cause: Privacy note added to GSVA skill was not included in nomogram-construction.
recommendations.fix: Add to Input Validation section: 'If your clinical CSV can be linked to patients or protected records, anonymize it before use. This workflow writes analysis data, model bundles, and session metadata to the local output_dir.'
